Fix scan failures: use -OutputFile to avoid PowerShell stream leaking to stdout (#69)

* Initial plan

* Fix scan failures: Write-Host leaks to stdout in non-interactive subprocess mode

Root cause: Write-Host in PowerShell uses the Information stream (stream 6),
which flows to the process's stdout when pwsh runs as a non-interactive
subprocess. Since bash redirects stdout to scan.json.tmp, any Write-Host
output prepends "WARNING:" text to the JSON, causing parse failures
('Unexpected character at position 0: W').

Fix: Replace all Write-Host diagnostic calls in Get-PrTriageData.ps1 with
[Console]::Error.WriteLine(), which writes directly to fd 2 (stderr).
Update ScriptHygiene.Tests.ps1 to ban Write-Host in Get-PrTriageData.ps1
and recommend [Console]::Error.WriteLine() instead.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/danmoseley/pr-dashboard/sessions/83d68ddc-2d80-43f6-b6a9-40c0ac151fb1

Co-authored-by: danmoseley <6385855+danmoseley@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update Write-Output test message to recommend [Console]::Error.WriteLine()

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use -OutputFile param instead of stdout redirection to fix stream leaking

PowerShell's Write-Warning (stream 3) and Write-Host (stream 6) leak to
stdout when pwsh runs as a subprocess from bash. Instead of working around
this with [Console]::Error.WriteLine(), the script now writes its output
file directly via -OutputFile, so stdout is never redirected and standard
PowerShell cmdlets work naturally.

- Add -OutputFile parameter; Emit-Output helper writes to file or stdout
- Revert [Console]::Error.WriteLine() back to idiomatic Write-Warning
- Use Write-Host for GH Actions ::warning:: annotations (needs exact prefix)
- Guard catch block against double-failure if file write fails
- Use UTF-8 without BOM for JSON output
- Update workflow to use -OutputFile instead of > redirect
- Replace Write-Warning/Write-Host bans with stdout-redirect ban in tests
- Update copilot-instructions.md local testing example

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix test -Because message to reflect -OutputFile approach

Enhanced CI/CD: 3 failure scenarios + build-test PR pipeline

Deploy pipeline (PowerGrid-Deploy):
- failure_scenario parameter: none, crash, perf, config, all
- crash: FORCE_ERROR=true on outage-api → 503 errors
- perf: SIMULATE_DELAY_MS=5000 on grid-status-api → 5s latency
- config: removes REQUIRED_CONFIG from notification-svc → crash loop
- all: injects all three simultaneously

CI pipeline (PowerGrid-BuildTest):
- Triggers on PRs to main
- Builds all services, runs inline tests
- Validates no failure env vars are set in code
- SRE Agent creates fix PR → CI runs → green → merge → clean deploy

End-to-end SRE Agent flow:
1. Run deploy with failure_scenario=crash
2. Alert fires → SRE Agent investigates
3. SRE Agent finds root cause via ADO pipeline run correlation
4. SRE Agent creates fix PR (CreateFixPullRequest tool)
5. CI pipeline validates the fix
6. Merge → deploy pipeline runs clean → services healthy

commit.message:
fix: login deadlock — Singleton provider with preferences fallback

ClaimsActiveUserProvider caused deadlocks in Blazor Server:
1. Scoped registration couldn't be resolved from Singleton repos (null)
2. AuthenticationStateProvider.GetAwaiter().GetResult() deadlocked the
   sync context

New approach: Singleton provider that uses HttpContext.User when
available (HTTP requests, prerender) and falls back to preferences
for Blazor SignalR calls. The login endpoint already writes
active_profile_id to preferences, so this value is current.

* feat: add SHA-256 hash verification and Windows Sandbox testing

  Introduce installer hash checking (verify command) and an end-to-end
  sandbox testing workflow (sandbox command) for generated PSADT packages.

  - Add optional sha256 field to Installer schema with hex validation
  - Add packager/hash.py for compute/verify with HashMismatchError
  - Capture InstallerSha256 from winget manifests in winget-scaffold
  - Add verify CLI command and auto-check in generate
  - Add packager/sandbox.py for .wsb config generation
  - Add sandbox_test.ps1.j2 and test_detection.ps1.j2 templates
  - Add sandbox CLI command with full install/detect/uninstall cycle
  - Update README.md and CLAUDE.md with new commands and fields

* Add markdown-driven package prototype for AI generation

Demonstrates simplified approach where:
- Markdown files with YAML frontmatter define packages

Follow-up to the resilience PR (85185fb). Regression surfaced when
scaffolding VSCodium: winget_scaffold.py crashed on `'NoneType' has no
attribute 'lower'`, and after fixing that the scaffolded .package.md
failed the new validator with 5 errors — because the scaffolder was

packager/winget.py:

  when the entry-level value is None. VSCodium's winget manifest
  declares InstallerType only at the top level, which crashed the

- Replaced [pscustomobject]@{...} with @{...} in Show-ADTInstallationWelcome
  CloseProcesses (v4 rejects pscustomobject here).

  `$installerPath = Join-Path -Path $PSScriptRoot -ChildPath 'Files\<filename>'`
  and a $installerPath variable reference on the Start-ADT* call.

  AND product_code matches a real MSI GUID. Winget often provides a

- For non-MSI installers with detection-only product codes, the scaffold
  now emits a TODO block pointing at `prototype.probe --emit-package-md`
  rather than a fraudulent MSI uninstall.

* fix: splicer must require language tag; scaffolder TODO now throws

Root cause of a silent uninstall failure reported against the VSCodium
package: the body-fence splicer was too permissive and inlined a plain
```fence (a shell command example from a scaffolded TODO block) as
PowerShell. The generated uninstall block contained the literal
command `uv run python -m packager.probe "VSCodium" --emit-package-md`,
which PSADT dutifully ran, captured the probe's stub output into the
log, and reported success — while VSCodium stayed installed.

packager/llm_generator.py (parse_markdown_sections):
- The language tag is now REQUIRED. Plain ``` fences are treated as
  prose/demo blocks and must not be spliced.
- Regex: `(?:powershell|ps1|pwsh)?` -> `(?:powershell|ps1|pwsh)`.

packager/winget_scaffold.py:
- The TODO block's shell-command example now uses ```text instead of
  a plain ``` fence (explicit intent, belt and braces — the stricter
  splicer would ignore either).
- The placeholder PowerShell block now `throw`s instead of calling
  `Write-ADTLogEntry -Severity 2`, so an incomplete package.md fails
  LOUDLY instead of silently "succeeding" at not uninstalling.

packages/vscodium.package.md:
- Replaced the TODO uninstall placeholder with a real block that reads
  UninstallString from HKCU:\...\{GUID}_is1 at runtime and runs the
  resolved `unins000.exe` with /VERYSILENT /SUPPRESSMSGBOXES /NORESTART.
  Handles both missing-app and missing-uninstaller edge cases.

tests/test_llm_generator.py (TestParseMarkdownSections):
- 5 new tests, including a direct regression test mirroring the
  VSCodium bug shape: a plain ``` fence + a ```powershell fence in
  the same section. The splicer must skip the plain one.

* feat: cache research findings and short-circuit winget-known packages

Before: every `generate` run made 3 Tavily API calls per package,
regardless of whether winget already had authoritative silent args,
and re-scaffolding the same package re-paid the full cost every time.
A batch of 20 packages burned 60 credits; most of them unnecessary.

After:
- Winget-known packages (Firefox, 7zip, VSCode, VSCodium, Slack, etc.)
  trigger a short-circuit in build_deployment_plan: if winget already
  provided silent args, or the installer is a plain MSI, search_vendor_docs
  is not called at all. The plan still gets a `tavily-skipped` marker
  finding so the LLM prompt shows the reasoning.
- When Tavily IS needed, search_vendor_docs is now ONE query (down from
  three): `<installer_filename> command line silent install uninstall`.
  Using the installer filename gives us version+vendor specificity for
  free, and naturally busts the cache on a version bump.
- Every Tavily call is wrapped by an on-disk JSON cache keyed on
  sha256(app_name|version|query). Repeated generations for the same
  (app, version) hit the cache and cost zero credits for 30 days.

packager/cache.py (new):
- ResearchCache with get/set/clear. Atomic writes via json.dumps.
- Cross-platform cache directory:
    * Windows: %LOCALAPPDATA%\packager\research-cache\
    * Linux/macOS: $XDG_CACHE_HOME/packager/research/ or ~/.cache/packager/research/
- TTL default 30 days, override via PACKAGER_RESEARCH_CACHE_TTL_DAYS.
- Cache location override via PACKAGER_RESEARCH_CACHE_DIR.
- Disable entirely with PACKAGER_NO_RESEARCH_CACHE=1.
- Every I/O path degrades to a silent no-op on failure so the cache
  never breaks generation.

packager/research.py:
- New _build_research_query() helper returns the single specific query.
- search_vendor_docs() looks up the cache first, writes back on miss.
- build_deployment_plan() skips search_vendor_docs() when winget has
  silent args or installer_kind == "msi"; override with
  PACKAGER_FORCE_WEB_RESEARCH=1.

tests/test_cache.py (new, 14 tests):
- Round-trip, miss-on-different-{app,version,query}, clear, clear on
  missing dir, TTL expiry, TTL env override (valid + invalid), disabled
  via env (get and set), cache dir env override, default dir shape.

tests/test_research.py (+5 tests):
- Cache hit on second search_vendor_docs call with Tavily mocked to
  count calls.
- Short-circuit fires when winget has silent args.
- Short-circuit does NOT fire without winget evidence.
- PACKAGER_FORCE_WEB_RESEARCH=1 bypasses the short-circuit.
- Existing parse-Tavily-json test now pins a tmp cache dir so it's
  hermetic.

Expected credit impact for a batch of 20 packages where 15 are
winget-known and 5 are custom:
  before: 60 Tavily calls
  after:   5 Tavily calls on the first run, 0 on subsequent runs
           (92% reduction, trending to 100% with warm cache)

Tests: 170 passing (+18: 14 cache + 5 research + 1 reshaped).

perf: isinstance fast path, lock-free StoreAttr, safe try elimination, global Arc dedup

- isinstance: add fast path in LoadGlobalCallFunction _ branch to handle
  isinstance(obj, cls) when the loaded global is the cls, not isinstance
  itself. Eliminates execute_one dispatch. (+133%, 3.9M → 9.1M ops/s)

- StoreAttr: replace intern_or_new() with direct map.get_mut() to avoid
  key allocation for existing attributes. (+27%, 7.9M → 10.0M ops/s)

- LoadGlobalStoreFast: skip Arc clone when destination local already holds
  the same global value (Arc::ptr_eq check). (+34%, 14.7M → 19.7M ops/s)

- Peephole: eliminate SetupFinally/PopBlock for try blocks with safe bodies
  (LOAD_CONST, STORE_FAST, etc. that cannot raise). (+17%, 11.1M → 13.0M)

commit.message:
fix: resolve two CI-only flaky test races in kernel

1. TestE2E_KillWait_RaceDetection timeout:
   twoPhaseShutdown goroutine was racing kern.Wait() to receive
   from proc.Done (buffered chan, cap=1). On slow CI runners,
   twoPhaseShutdown would steal the one-shot exit value, leaving
   Wait() blocked forever.

   Fix: add proc.terminated (chan struct{}) closed by Terminate()
   and closeTerminated(). twoPhaseShutdown now listens on
   proc.terminated (broadcast, readable by N goroutines) so only
   kern.Wait() consumes from proc.Done.

2. TestATDD27_1 TempDir RemoveAll cleanup failure:
   asyncWriteCheckpoint launched fire-and-forget goroutines not
   tracked in proc.wg. After tests read proc.Done the process
   appeared done, but the checkpoint goroutine could still be
   writing files. When k.Shutdown() ran (via defer) and then
   Go's TempDir cleanup tried to delete the dir, the goroutine
   was still writing — causing 'directory not empty'.

   Fix: track checkpoint goroutines in proc.wg via wg.Go().
   Also extend Shutdown() to cancel and drain all process wgs,
   ensuring no background file I/O outlives the kernel lifetime.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add delta encoding for IntershardEntityUpdate

New message type INTERSHARD_ENTITY_UPDATE_DELTA (0x0314) with variable-
length wire format: entity_id(4B) + seq(2B) + bitfield(2B) + changed fields.

Includes encode/decode functions and bitfield computation in delta module.
Typical update (only x changed) is 10 bytes vs 40 bytes full update.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
